tests/test_net.c.notes: Socket runtime checks and showcase/example execution are opt-in because they are expensive and can trigger firewall prompts. Default regression still compiles the shard.

l_term_raw.notes: Smoke-covered together with l_term_restore using a zero-length nonblocking read to avoid blocking.

l_term_restore.notes: Exercised immediately after l_term_raw so terminal mode is restored in the same test.

l_read_nonblock.notes: Zero-length read path is deterministic across platforms and verifies immediate return.

l_term_size.notes: Asserts positive, sane dimensions without depending on an exact console size.

l_localtime.notes: Validated against plausible timezone offsets everywhere and exact TZ parsing on non-Windows.

l_execve.notes: Still exercised via l_spawn and l_spawn_stdio child exec paths; direct exec would replace the test process.

l_poll.notes: Default regression covers pipe readiness without opening sockets; socket-backed poll flows remain in manual test_net runtime checks.

l_canvas_alive.notes: Backend helper state is exercised with synthetic canvases.

l_canvas_key.notes: Windows key queue behavior is covered with a synthetic queued key.

l_canvas_mouse.notes: Cached mouse state path is covered without requiring a live device.

l_canvas_open.notes: Left intentionally uncovered in CI because it requires a live framebuffer or windowing environment.

l_canvas_flush.notes: Left intentionally uncovered in CI because it requires a live framebuffer or windowing environment.

l_base64_encode.notes: Tested via RFC 4648 vectors, round-trip, binary data, URL-safe aliases, whitespace skip, and error paths.

l_base64_decode.notes: Tested via RFC 4648 vectors, round-trip, binary data, URL-safe aliases, whitespace skip, and error paths.
